original_hypothesis.title: Refund Deadline Modification Permanently Locks Funds
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The ILOManager owner can arbitrarily change the `refundDeadline` to `type(uint64).max`, permanently blocking the refund path for all investors if a project fails to launch.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: If a project fails to launch (e.g., due to soft cap not met or price manipulation), investors rely on the refund mechanism. A compromised or malicious owner can lock these funds forever by pushing the deadline infinitely into the future.

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner sets deadline to max uint64, preventing refundable() modifier from passing
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Extortion of investors or project admins

economic_questions: Is there any timelock on this owner action?
Can this be used to force a launch under unfavorable conditions?
validation_requirements: Verify owner can call this during an active sale

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When pool fails to meet soft cap
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Total loss of trust and locked funds

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
